source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Consider a baseband binary PAM receiver shown in <image>. The additive channel noise \( n(t) \) has a power spectral density \( S_N(f) = \frac{N_0}{2} = 10^{-20} \text{ W/Hz} \). The lowpass filter is ideal with unity gain and cut-off frequency 1 MHz. Let \( Y_k \) represent the random variable \( y(t_k) \).

\[
Y_k = \begin{cases} 
N_k & \text{if transmitted bit } b_k = 0 \\
a + N_k & \text{if transmitted bit } b_k = 1 
\end{cases}
\]

where \( N_k \) represents the noise sample value. The noise sample has a probability density function \( P_{N_k}(n) = 0.5 \alpha e^{-\alpha|n|} \) (This has mean zero and variance \( \frac{2}{\alpha^2} \)). Assume transmitted bits are equiprobable and the threshold \( z \) is set to \( \frac{a}{2} = 10^{-6} \text{ V} \).

The probability of bit error is

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'receiver shown in the figure'
however, the text already provides the key equations, noise model, threshold, and answer choices, so the figure may be supportive rather than strictly necessary
multi_step: solving requires combining the PSD and lowpass cutoff to get output noise variance
the variance must be linked to the Laplacian parameter alpha via variance = 2/alpha^2
the final bit-error probability then requires applying the threshold decision rule and Laplacian tail probability
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the target answer is a precise closed-form expression
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward answer checking
the quantities needed for verification are numerically specified in the text, and registry_passed=true

asset_records.normalized_text_snapshot:
Consider a baseband binary PAM receiver shown in the figure. The additive channel noise \(n(t)\) has a power spectral density \(S_N(f) = \frac{N_0}{2} = 10^{-20}\ \text{W/Hz}\). The lowpass filter is ideal with unity gain and cut-off frequency 1 MHz. Let \(Y_k\) represent the random variable \(y(t_k)\).

\[
Y_k = \begin{cases}
N_k & \text{if transmitted bit } b_k = 0 \\
a + N_k & \text{if transmitted bit } b_k = 1
\end{cases}
\]

where \(N_k\) represents the noise sample value. The noise sample has a probability density function \(P_{N_k}(n) = 0.5\alpha e^{-\alpha|n|}\) (this has mean zero and variance \(\frac{2}{\alpha^2}\)). Assume transmitted bits are equiprobable and the threshold \(z\) is set to \(\frac{a}{2} = 10^{-6}\ \text{V}\). What is the probability of bit error?

Assume transmitted bits are equiprobable and the threshold \( z \) is set to \( \frac{a}{2} = 10^{-6} \text{ V} \).

rewrite_reports.rationale: The stem already asks for a computed value; removing the options and turning the ending into a direct question preserves the original task.

variants.title: Dataset Open Question
variants.rewritten_question_text:
Consider a baseband binary PAM receiver shown in the figure. The additive channel noise \(n(t)\) has a power spectral density \(S_N(f) = \frac{N_0}{2} = 10^{-20}\ \text{W/Hz}\). The lowpass filter is ideal with unity gain and cut-off frequency 1 MHz. Let \(Y_k\) represent the random variable \(y(t_k)\).

\[
Y_k = \begin{cases}
N_k & \text{if transmitted bit } b_k = 0 \\
a + N_k & \text{if transmitted bit } b_k = 1
\end{cases}
\]

where \(N_k\) represents the noise sample value. The noise sample has a probability density function \(P_{N_k}(n) = 0.5\alpha e^{-\alpha|n|}\) (this has mean zero and variance \(\frac{2}{\alpha^2}\)). Assume transmitted bits are equiprobable and the threshold \(z\) is set to \(\frac{a}{2} = 10^{-6}\ \text{V}\). What is the probability of bit error?

where \(N_k\) represents the noise sample value.
